skill.name: brainstorming
skill.description: Guides collaborative brainstorming before implementation by reviewing project context, asking focused questions, comparing approaches, and documenting a validated design.
skill.summary: Turns early feature ideas into validated design plans before implementation.
skill.author: davila7

security_audit.summary: The static analyzer reported one external-command pattern and 13 weak-cryptography blockers. The weak-cryptography reports are false positives from prose, and the backtick shell report is Markdown inline code. The skill is low risk because it asks the assistant to inspect project context, write a design document, and commit it to git.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography Static Findings Are Prose False Positives
low_findings.description: The analyzer flagged weak cryptography on multiple SKILL.md lines, but the file contains planning instructions only. No cryptographic algorithm, encryption routine, hashing call, or key handling evidence was found.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited locations are natural-language planning guidance, not executable code. I found no evidence of crypto APIs, algorithms, secrets, or encoded payloads.

low_findings.title: Markdown Backtick Misclassified as Shell Execution
low_findings.description: The analyzer flagged SKILL.md line 38 as Ruby or shell backtick execution. The backticks are Markdown formatting around a documentation path, so this is not executable shell syntax.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The backticks appear inside a Markdown bullet and wrap docs/plans/YYYY-MM-DD-<topic>-design.md. There is no script file or command interpreter context.

low_findings.title: Repository File and Git Workflow Instructions
low_findings.description: The skill asks the assistant to inspect project state, write a design document, and commit it to git. This is expected for the workflow, but it can change files and repository history.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The instructions explicitly mention checking files, writing to docs/plans, and committing to git. The behavior is legitimate but has observable filesystem and git side effects.

content.value_statement: Early feature ideas often miss constraints, trade-offs, and success criteria. This skill guides Claude, Codex, or Claude Code through focused discovery and a validated design plan.
actual_capabilities: Reviews project context through files, documentation, and recent commits.
Asks one focused question at a time to clarify goals and constraints.
Compares two or three implementation approaches with trade-offs.
Presents design sections covering architecture, data flow, errors, and tests.
Writes the validated design to a dated document under docs/plans.
Can prepare the user for a follow-up implementation planning workflow.
limitations: Does not implement the feature by itself.
Requires user responses to resolve ambiguous product decisions.
May create or commit documentation when the user approves that workflow.
Depends on separate skills for git worktrees and detailed implementation plans.

use_cases.title: Shape a New Feature
use_cases.description: Turn a rough feature request into clear goals, constraints, alternatives, and success criteria before engineering starts.

use_cases.title: Design a Technical Change
use_cases.description: Explore architecture options, data flow, error handling, and testing strategy before changing production code.

use_cases.title: Align a Team on Scope
use_cases.description: Create a shared design document that captures decisions, trade-offs, and remaining questions for team review.

prompt_templates.title: Brainstorm a Small Feature
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner discovery for a simple product idea.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the brainstorming skill to help me shape this feature idea: [describe the idea]. Ask one question at a time.

prompt_templates.title: Compare Implementation Options
prompt_templates.scenario: Intermediate planning when several approaches are possible.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the brainstorming skill to compare two or three approaches for [feature]. Include trade-offs, constraints, and a recommendation.

prompt_templates.title: Create a Validated Design
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced planning for a feature that needs a written design.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the brainstorming skill to design [feature]. Review project context, clarify requirements, then present the design in validated sections.

prompt_templates.title: Prepare for Implementation
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the brainstorming skill to finalize the design for [feature], document it under docs/plans, and prepare next steps for implementation planning.

output_examples.input: Design an onboarding checklist for new workspace users.
output_examples.output: A focused question about the user journey, followed by approach options and a scoped design covering data, UI states, and tests.

output_examples.input: Plan a retry system for failed background jobs.
output_examples.output: A comparison of retry strategies, a recommended design, error handling notes, observability needs, and validation checkpoints.

output_examples.input: Help define requirements for team-level permissions.
output_examples.output: A sequence of single questions, clarified roles, permission boundaries, risks, and a design document outline.

best_practices: Provide the rough idea, target users, and known constraints before starting.
Answer each question directly so the design can stay focused.
Review each design section before approving documentation or git changes.
anti_patterns: Do not use it when requirements are already final and implementation should start immediately.
Do not skip the question phase for complex or ambiguous ideas.
Do not approve commits without reviewing the generated design document.

faq.question: What does this skill do?
faq.answer: It guides collaborative brainstorming and turns early ideas into validated design plans.

faq.question: Does it write production code?
faq.answer: No. It focuses on discovery, design, documentation, and preparation for implementation.

faq.question: Why does it ask one question at a time?
faq.answer: One question reduces confusion and helps clarify requirements without overwhelming the user.

faq.question: Can it create files?
faq.answer: Yes. The skill can write a validated design document under docs/plans when that workflow is approved.

faq.question: Can it use git?
faq.answer: Yes. The skill can request a git commit for the design document, so users should review changes first.

faq.question: Which tools can use it?
faq.answer: The report lists support for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.
